titleEn: Croplands expands WEED-IT range with new releases, including orchard solution
titleZh: Croplands扩展WEED-IT精准喷雾系列：推出果园专用方案及新一代自主喷雾平台
summaryZh: 澳大利亚Croplands Equipment公司发布WEED-IT精准喷雾系列多项新技术，包括与SwarmFarm Robotics合作的Series 2 Robot Ready Pinto自主喷雾平台、36米桁架式大型大田喷雾机Pegasus，以及首款专为果园设计的光学点喷方案。同时，其WEED-IT改装套件已成功适配多品牌喷雾机，展示出强大的兼容性和实践价值。
translationZh:
澳大利亚公司Croplands Equipment在其WEED-IT精准喷雾系列中推出了一系列新技术，其中主打产品是Series 2 Robot Ready Pinto，以及一款新的大型大田喷雾平台、扩展的改装能力，以及公司首款专为果园设计的光学点喷解决方案。

引领这些新产品的便是Series 2 Robot Ready Pinto，这是Croplands自主喷雾平台的下一代产品。该机具结合了SwarmFarm Robotics的SwarmBot、WEED-IT光学点喷技术以及经过验证的Pinto平台，使种植者能够实现喷雾作业自动化，同时最大化精度和效率——从而节省化学品用量，并在化学药剂施用方面获得更大灵活性。

Series 2对该平台进行了针对性的工程改进，包括升级的罐体设计以改善重心，以及一系列为满足自主田间作业需求而开发的进一步增强功能。

Croplands WEED-IT产品负责人Lachlan Coom表示，此次更新反映了种植者的反馈以及自主喷雾市场日益成熟的趋势。他说：“Series 2 Robot Ready Pinto建立在种植者已经信赖的平台之上，并加入了专门的设计改进。”

**新Pegasus平台**

与Series 2同时推出的，还有Croplands为其著名的Pegasus 7000L喷雾机配备的一款专为WEED-IT设计的36米桁架式喷杆。其结果是形成了一款更紧凑的大型大田喷雾机，具备双罐和双喷雾管路能力，使农民能够拥有一台既能进行点喷、又能进行全面喷洒，或同时进行两种作业的多功能机器。

该平台在发布前已经过过去两年的大量田间测试。Coom表示：“我们对这次发布感到兴奋。这是为了提供能够融入实际农业系统的实用解决方案，同时让操作者在如何以及何时进行喷雾方面拥有更大的灵活性。”

围绕Croplands的WEED-IT改装套件，势头也日益强劲。该公司的工程和现场服务团队已成功在多种品牌的喷雾机上安装了光学点喷传感器。来自Case Patriot、John Deere、Goldacres、Fendt Rogator、Hayes和Hardi的自走式、悬挂式和牵引式设备均已成功改装，这进一步证明了WEED-IT系统的适应性。

Coom表示：“我们处理过各种各样的机器，到目前为止，还没有遇到我们无法改装的喷雾机。种植者可以用WEED-IT技术升级现有设备，无需投资购买全新的机器，就能开始看到节省效果。”
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了精准农业技术在种植业（尤其是大田和果园）中的最新集成应用，其自主化、光学点喷和广泛改装能力对智慧畜牧行业中的饲料作物种植、牧场杂草管理等场景具有直接借鉴意义。
contentFull: Croplands expands WEED-IT range with new releases, including orchard solution 18-06 | Smart farming | NewsCombining SwarmFarm Robotics’ SwarmBot, WEED-IT optical spot spraying technology and the proven Pinto platform, the new implement enables growers to automate spraying operations while maximising accuracy and efficiency – delivering chemical savings and greater flexibility in chemistry application. Photo: CroplandsAustralian company Croplands Equipment has unveiled a wave of new technology across its WEED-IT precision spraying range, headlined by the Series 2 Robot Ready Pinto and spanning a new large-scale broadacre platform, expanded retrofit capability and the company’s first optical spot spraying solution purpose-built for orchards.Leading the new releases is the Series 2 Robot Ready Pinto, the next generation of Croplands’ autonomous spraying platform. Combining SwarmFarm Robotics’ SwarmBot, WEED-IT optical spot spraying technology and the proven Pinto platform, the new implement enables growers to automate spraying operations while maximising accuracy and efficiency – delivering chemical savings and greater flexibility in chemistry application.The Series 2 brings targeted engineering refinements to the platform, including an upgraded tank design for improved centre of gravity, along with a suite of further enhancements developed for the demands of autonomous field operation.Croplands WEED-IT Product Lead Lachlan Coom says the update reflects both grower feedback and the growing maturity of the autonomous spraying market. “The Series 2 Robot Ready Pinto builds on a platform that growers already trust, with dedicated design enhancements.”New Pegasus platform Alongside the Series 2, Croplands is releasing a purpose-built WEED-IT-ready 36-metre truss boom mounted to its renowned Pegasus 7000L sprayer. The result is a more compact large-scale broadacre sprayer with twin tank and twin spray line capabilities, giving farmers a versatile machine capable of spot spraying, blanket spraying or both simultaneously.The platform has been extensively field tested over the past two years ahead of its release. “We are excited about the launch”, says Coom. “It’s about delivering practical solutions that fit into real farming systems, while giving operators more flexibility in how and when they spray.”Strong momentum is also building around Croplands’ WEED-IT Retrofit Kits, with the company’s engineering and field service teams having successfully installed optical spot spraying sensors across a wide range of sprayer brands. Self-propelled, linkage and trailed units from Case Patriot, John Deere, Goldacres, Fendt Rogator, Hayes and Hardi have all been retrofitted, reinforcing the adaptability of the WEED-IT system.Coom: “We’ve worked on a broad mix of machines and so far, we haven’t come across a sprayer we couldn’t retrofit. Growers can upgrade existing equipment with WEED-IT technology and start seeing savings without needing to invest in a completely new unit.”WEED-IT heading to the orchardThose savings are driven by WEED-IT’s ability to significantly reduce chemical use, improve spraying efficiency and help combat herbicide resistance in hard-to-control weeds. The technology’s credentials are well established: over the past 15 years, Croplands has installed more than 25,000 WEED-IT sensors, primarily in broadacre applications where the system has redefined spraying practices and supported more sustainable farming outcomes.Now, that same capability is being extended to horticulture with the introduction of the WEED-IT Orchard 4000 – the company’s first full-row optical spot spraying solution designed specifically for orchard weed control. The system delivers targeted herbicide application across entire rows, bringing the precision spraying benefits long available to broadacre growers to the tree crop market for the first time.Supporting the full range is the WEED-IT DASH 10″ touchscreen, designed to give operators greater confidence and insight into their spraying performance, with remote upgrade and support capability built in. Croplands will have WEED-IT equipment and technology on display across several upcoming field days in Australia.Join 17,000+ subscribersSubscribe to our newsletter to stay updated about all the need-to-know content in the agricultural sector, two times a week. author: René Groeneveld
contentHtml: Australian company Croplands Equipment has unveiled a wave of new technology across its WEED-IT precision spraying range, headlined by the Series 2 Robot Ready Pinto and spanning a new large-scale broadacre platform, expanded retrofit capability and the company’s first optical spot spraying solution purpose-built for orchards.
